    66→def create_plan_header_block(
    67→    *,
    68→    created_at: str,
    69→    created_by: str,
    70→    worktree_name: str | None,
    71→    branch_name: str | None,
    72→    plan_comment_id: int | None,
    73→    last_dispatched_run_id: str | None,
    74→    last_dispatched_node_id: str | None,
    75→    last_dispatched_at: str | None,
    76→    last_local_impl_at: str | None,
    77→    last_local_impl_event: str | None,
    78→    last_local_impl_session: str | None,
    79→    last_local_impl_user: str | None,
    80→    last_remote_impl_at: str | None,
    81→    last_remote_impl_run_id: str | None,
    82→    last_remote_impl_session_id: str | None,
    83→    source_repo: str | None,
    84→    objective_issue: int | None,
    85→    created_from_session: str | None,
    86→    created_from_workflow_run_url: str | None,
    87→    last_learn_session: str | None,
    88→    last_learn_at: str | None,
    89→    learn_status: LearnStatusValue | None,
    90→    learn_plan_issue: int | None,
    91→    learn_plan_pr: int | None,
    92→    learned_from_issue: int | None,
    93→    lifecycle_stage: str | None,
    94→) -> MetadataBlock:
    95→    """Create a plan-header metadata block with validation.
    96→
    97→    Args:
    98→        created_at: ISO 8601 timestamp of plan creation
    99→        created_by: GitHub username of plan creator
   100→        worktree_name: Optional worktree name (set when worktree is created)
   101→        branch_name: Optional git branch name for this plan
   102→        plan_comment_id: Optional GitHub comment ID containing plan content
   103→        last_dispatched_run_id: Optional workflow run ID (set by workflow)
   104→        last_dispatched_node_id: Optional GraphQL node ID (set by workflow, for batch queries)
   105→        last_dispatched_at: Optional dispatch timestamp (set by workflow)
   106→        last_local_impl_at: Optional local implementation timestamp (set by plan-implement)
   107→        last_local_impl_event: Optional event type ("started" or "ended")
   108→        last_local_impl_session: Optional Claude Code session ID
   109→        last_local_impl_user: Optional user who ran implementation
   110→        last_remote_impl_at: Optional remote implementation timestamp (set by GitHub Actions)
   111→        last_remote_impl_run_id: Optional GitHub Actions run ID for remote implementation
   112→        last_remote_impl_session_id: Optional Claude Code session ID for remote implementation
   113→        source_repo: For cross-repo plans, the repo where implementation happens
   114→        objective_issue: Optional parent objective issue number
   115→        created_from_session: Optional session ID that created this plan
   116→        created_from_workflow_run_url: Optional workflow run URL that created this plan
   117→        last_learn_session: Optional session ID that last invoked learn
   118→        last_learn_at: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp of last learn invocation
   119→        learn_status: Optional learning workflow status
   120→        learn_plan_issue: Optional issue number of generated learn plan
   121→        learn_plan_pr: Optional PR number that implemented the learn plan
   122→        learned_from_issue: Optional parent plan issue number (for learn plans)
   123→        lifecycle_stage: Optional lifecycle stage for the plan
   124→
   125→    Returns:
   126→        MetadataBlock with plan-header schema
   127→    """
   128→    schema = PlanHeaderSchema()
   129→
   130→    data: dict[str, Any] = {
   131→        SCHEMA_VERSION: "2",
   132→        CREATED_AT: created_at,
   133→        CREATED_BY: created_by,
   134→        PLAN_COMMENT_ID: plan_comment_id,
   135→        LAST_DISPATCHED_RUN_ID: last_dispatched_run_id,
   136→        LAST_DISPATCHED_NODE_ID: last_dispatched_node_id,
   137→        LAST_DISPATCHED_AT: last_dispatched_at,
   138→        LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT: last_local_impl_at,
   139→        LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_EVENT: last_local_impl_event,
   140→        LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_SESSION: last_local_impl_session,
   141→        LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_USER: last_local_impl_user,
   142→        LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT: last_remote_impl_at,
   143→        LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_RUN_ID: last_remote_impl_run_id,
   144→        LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_SESSION_ID: last_remote_impl_session_id,
   145→    }
   146→    # Only include worktree_name if provided
   147→    if worktree_name is not None:
   148→        data[WORKTREE_NAME] = worktree_name
   149→
   150→    # Only include branch_name if provided
   151→    if branch_name is not None:
   152→        data[BRANCH_NAME] = branch_name
   153→
   154→    # Include source_repo for cross-repo plans
   155→    if source_repo is not None:
   156→        data[SOURCE_REPO] = source_repo
   157→
   158→    # Include objective_issue if provided
   159→    if objective_issue is not None:
   160→        data[OBJECTIVE_ISSUE] = objective_issue
   161→
   162→    # Include created_from_session if provided
   163→    if created_from_session is not None:
   164→        data[CREATED_FROM_SESSION] = created_from_session
   165→
   166→    # Include created_from_workflow_run_url if provided
   167→    if created_from_workflow_run_url is not None:
   168→        data[CREATED_FROM_WORKFLOW_RUN_URL] = created_from_workflow_run_url
   169→
   170→    # Include last_learn_session if provided
   171→    if last_learn_session is not None:
   172→        data[LAST_LEARN_SESSION] = last_learn_session
   173→
   174→    # Include last_learn_at if provided
   175→    if last_learn_at is not None:
   176→        data[LAST_LEARN_AT] = last_learn_at
   177→
   178→    # Include learn_status if provided
   179→    if learn_status is not None:
   180→        data[LEARN_STATUS] = learn_status
   181→
   182→    # Include learn_plan_issue if provided
   183→    if learn_plan_issue is not None:
   184→        data[LEARN_PLAN_ISSUE] = learn_plan_issue
   185→
   186→    # Include learn_plan_pr if provided
   187→    if learn_plan_pr is not None:
   188→        data[LEARN_PLAN_PR] = learn_plan_pr
   189→
   190→    # Include learned_from_issue if provided
   191→    if learned_from_issue is not None:
   192→        data[LEARNED_FROM_ISSUE] = learned_from_issue
   193→
   194→    # Include lifecycle_stage if provided
   195→    if lifecycle_stage is not None:
   196→        data[LIFECYCLE_STAGE] = lifecycle_stage
   197→
   198→    return create_metadata_block(
   199→        key=schema.get_key(),
   200→        data=data,
   201→        schema=schema,
   202→    )
   203→

   207→    created_at: str,

   210→    branch_name: str | None,
   211→    plan_comment_id: int | None,
   212→    last_dispatched_run_id: str | None,

   214→    last_dispatched_at: str | None,
   215→    last_local_impl_at: str | None,
   216→    last_local_impl_event: str | None,
   217→    last_local_impl_session: str | None,
   218→    last_local_impl_user: str | None,
   219→    last_remote_impl_at: str | None,
   220→    last_remote_impl_run_id: str | None,
   221→    last_remote_impl_session_id: str | None,
   222→    source_repo: str | None,
   223→    objective_issue: int | None,

   234→    """Format issue body with only metadata (schema version 2).

   245→        last_dispatched_run_id: Optional workflow run ID

   247→        last_dispatched_at: Optional dispatch timestamp
   248→        last_local_impl_at: Optional local implementation timestamp
   249→        last_local_impl_event: Optional event type ("started" or "ended")
   250→        last_local_impl_session: Optional Claude Code session ID
   251→        last_local_impl_user: Optional user who ran implementation
   252→        last_remote_impl_at: Optional remote implementation timestamp
   253→        last_remote_impl_run_id: Optional GitHub Actions run ID for remote implementation
   254→        last_remote_impl_session_id: Optional Claude Code session ID for remote implementation
   255→        source_repo: For cross-repo plans, the repo where implementation happens
   256→        objective_issue: Optional parent objective issue number
   257→        created_from_session: Optional session ID that created this plan
   258→        created_from_workflow_run_url: Optional workflow run URL that created this plan
   259→        last_learn_session: Optional session ID that last invoked learn
   260→        last_learn_at: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp of last learn invocation
   261→        learn_status: Optional learning workflow status
   262→        learn_plan_issue: Optional issue number of generated learn plan
   263→        learn_plan_pr: Optional PR number that implemented the learn plan
   264→        learned_from_issue: Optional parent plan issue number (for learn plans)
   265→        lifecycle_stage: Optional lifecycle stage for the plan
   266→
   267→    Returns:
   268→        Issue body string with metadata block only
   269→    """
   270→    block = create_plan_header_block(
   271→        created_at=created_at,
   272→        created_by=created_by,
   273→        worktree_name=worktree_name,
   274→        branch_name=branch_name,
   275→        plan_comment_id=plan_comment_id,
   276→        last_dispatched_run_id=last_dispatched_run_id,
   277→        last_dispatched_node_id=last_dispatched_node_id,
   278→        last_dispatched_at=last_dispatched_at,
   279→        last_local_impl_at=last_local_impl_at,
   280→        last_local_impl_event=last_local_impl_event,
   281→        last_local_impl_session=last_local_impl_session,
   282→        last_local_impl_user=last_local_impl_user,
   283→        last_remote_impl_at=last_remote_impl_at,
   284→        last_remote_impl_run_id=last_remote_impl_run_id,
   285→        last_remote_impl_session_id=last_remote_impl_session_id,
   286→        source_repo=source_repo,
   287→        objective_issue=objective_issue,
   288→        created_from_session=created_from_session,
   289→        created_from_workflow_run_url=created_from_workflow_run_url,
   290→        last_learn_session=last_learn_session,
   291→        last_learn_at=last_learn_at,
   292→        learn_status=learn_status,
   293→        learn_plan_issue=learn_plan_issue,
   294→        learn_plan_pr=learn_plan_pr,
   295→        learned_from_issue=learned_from_issue,
   296→        lifecycle_stage=lifecycle_stage,
   297→    )
   298→
   299→    return render_metadata_block(block)

   316→
   317→def extract_plan_from_comment(comment_body: str) -> str | None:
   318→    """Extract plan content from a comment with plan-body metadata block.
   319→
   320→    Extracts from both:
   321→    - New format: <!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-body --> with <details>
   322→    - Old format: <!-- erk:plan-content --> (backward compatibility)
   323→
   324→    Args:
   325→        comment_body: Comment body potentially containing plan content
   326→
   327→    Returns:
   328→        Extracted plan content, or None if markers not found
   329→    """
   330→    # Import here to avoid circular dependency
   331→    from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import extract_raw_metadata_blocks
   332→
   333→    # Try new format first (plan-body metadata block)
   334→    raw_blocks = extract_raw_metadata_blocks(comment_body)
   335→    for block in raw_blocks:
   336→        if block.key == "plan-body":
   337→            # Extract content from <details> structure
   338→            # The plan-body block uses <strong> tags in summary (not <code>)
   339→            # Accept both <details> and <details open>
   340→            pattern = r"<details(?:\s+open)?>\s*<summary>.*?</summary>\s*(.*?)\s*</details>"
   341→            match = re.search(pattern, block.body, re.DOTALL)
   342→            if match:
   343→                return match.group(1).strip()
   344→
   345→    # Fall back to old format (backward compatibility)
   346→    pattern = r"<!-- erk:plan-content -->\s*(.*?)\s*<!-- /erk:plan-content -->"
   347→    match = re.search(pattern, comment_body, re.DOTALL)
   348→
   349→    if match is None:
   350→        return None
   351→
   352→    return match.group(1).strip()
   353→

   355→def update_plan_header_dispatch(
   356→    issue_body: str,
   357→    run_id: str,
   358→    node_id: str,
   359→    dispatched_at: str,
   360→) -> str:
   361→    """Update dispatch fields in plan-header metadata block.
   362→
   363→    Uses Python YAML parsing for robustness (not regex).
   364→    This function reads the existing plan-header block, updates the
   365→    dispatch fields, and re-renders the entire body.
   366→
   367→    Args:
   368→        issue_body: Current issue body containing plan-header block
   369→        run_id: Workflow run ID to set
   370→        node_id: GraphQL node ID to set (for batch queries)
   371→        dispatched_at: ISO 8601 timestamp of dispatch
   372→
   373→    Returns:
   374→        Updated issue body with new dispatch fields
   375→
   376→    Raises:
   377→        ValueError: If plan-header block not found or invalid
   378→    """
   379→    # Extract existing plan-header block
   380→    block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, "plan-header")
   381→    if block is None:
   382→        raise ValueError("plan-header block not found in issue body")
   383→
   384→    # Update dispatch fields
   385→    updated_data = dict(block.data)
   386→    updated_data[LAST_DISPATCHED_RUN_ID] = run_id
   387→    updated_data[LAST_DISPATCHED_NODE_ID] = node_id
   388→    updated_data[LAST_DISPATCHED_AT] = dispatched_at
   389→
   390→    # Validate updated data
   391→    schema = PlanHeaderSchema()
   392→    schema.validate(updated_data)
   393→
   394→    # Create new block and render
   395→    new_block = MetadataBlock(key="plan-header", data=updated_data)
   396→    new_block_content = render_metadata_block(new_block)
   397→
   398→    # Replace block in full body
   399→    return replace_metadata_block_in_body(issue_body, "plan-header", new_block_content)
   400→

   407→    Args:

   409→

   420→    dispatched_at = block.data.get(LAST_DISPATCHED_AT)

   422→    return (run_id, node_id, dispatched_at)

   596→def update_plan_header_worktree_and_branch(
   597→    *,
   598→    issue_body: str,
   599→    worktree_name: str,
   600→    branch_name: str,
   601→) -> str:
   602→    """Update worktree_name and branch_name fields in plan-header metadata block.
   603→
   604→    Sets both fields atomically in a single update. This is called when
   605→    implementation starts to record which worktree and branch are being used.
   606→
   607→    Args:
   608→        issue_body: Current issue body containing plan-header block
   609→        worktree_name: The worktree name to set
   610→        branch_name: The git branch name to set
   611→
   612→    Returns:
   613→        Updated issue body with new worktree_name and branch_name fields
   614→
   615→    Raises:
   616→        ValueError: If plan-header block not found or invalid
   617→    """
   618→    # Extract existing plan-header block
   619→    block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, "plan-header")
   620→    if block is None:
   621→        raise ValueError("plan-header block not found in issue body")
   622→
   623→    # Update both fields atomically
   624→    updated_data = dict(block.data)

  1231→
  1232→
  1233→def update_plan_header_learn_result(
  1234→    *,
  1235→    issue_body: str,
  1236→    learn_status: LearnStatusValue,
  1237→    learn_plan_issue: int | None,
  1238→    learn_plan_pr: int | None,
  1239→) -> str:
  1240→    """Update learn_status and optionally learn_plan_issue/learn_plan_pr atomically.
  1241→
  1242→    This is called when learn workflow completes to record the result:
  1243→    - "completed_no_plan": Learn completed but no plan was needed
  1244→    - "completed_with_plan": Learn completed and created a plan (learn_plan_issue set)
  1245→    - "pending_review": Documentation PR created directly (learn_plan_pr set)
  1246→
  1247→    Args:
  1248→        issue_body: Current issue body containing plan-header block
  1249→        learn_status: New learn status value
  1250→        learn_plan_issue: Issue number of created plan (for completed_with_plan)
  1251→        learn_plan_pr: PR number of documentation PR (for pending_review)
  1252→
  1253→    Returns:
  1254→        Updated issue body with new fields
  1255→
  1256→    Raises:
  1257→        ValueError: If plan-header block not found or invalid
  1258→    """
  1259→    # Extract existing plan-header block
  1260→    block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, "plan-header")
  1261→    if block is None:
  1262→        raise ValueError("plan-header block not found in issue body")
  1263→
  1264→    # Update fields atomically
  1265→    updated_data = dict(block.data)
  1266→    updated_data[LEARN_STATUS] = learn_status
  1267→    if learn_plan_issue is not None:
  1268→        updated_data[LEARN_PLAN_ISSUE] = learn_plan_issue
  1269→    if learn_plan_pr is not None:
  1270→        updated_data[LEARN_PLAN_PR] = learn_plan_pr
  1271→
  1272→    # Validate updated data
  1273→    schema = PlanHeaderSchema()
  1274→    schema.validate(updated_data)
  1275→
  1276→    # Create new block and render
  1277→    new_block = MetadataBlock(key="plan-header", data=updated_data)
  1278→    new_block_content = render_metadata_block(new_block)
  1279→
  1280→    # Replace block in full body
  1281→    return replace_metadata_block_in_body(issue_body, "plan-header", new_block_content)
  1282→
  1283→
  1284→def update_plan_header_learn_plan_completed(
  1285→    *,
  1286→    issue_body: str,
  1287→    learn_plan_pr: int,
  1288→) -> str:
  1289→    """Set learn_status to plan_completed and record the PR number.
  1290→

  1292→    plan issue that generated it.
  1293→
  1294→    Args:
  1295→        issue_body: Current issue body containing plan-header block
  1296→        learn_plan_pr: PR number that implemented the learn plan
  1297→
  1298→    Returns:
  1299→        Updated issue body with status set to plan_completed
  1300→
  1301→    Raises:

  1303→    """
  1304→    # Extract existing plan-header block

  1310→    updated_data = dict(block.data)
  1311→    updated_data[LEARN_STATUS] = "plan_completed"
  1312→    updated_data[LEARN_PLAN_PR] = learn_plan_pr
  1313→
  1314→    # Validate updated data

  1317→
  1318→    # Create new block and render

"""Schema v2 plan header operations for erk-plan issues.

These support the new plan issue structure where:
- Issue body contains only compact metadata (for fast querying)
- First comment contains the plan content
- last_dispatched_run_id is stored in issue body

Optional fields (added over time, backward compatible):
- source_repo: For cross-repo plans, the repo where this plan will be
  implemented (e.g., "owner/impl-repo"). When set, the plan issue lives in
  a different repo (the plans repo) than where code changes will be made.
"""

import re
from typing import Any

from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import (
    create_metadata_block,
    create_plan_body_block,
    find_metadata_block,
    render_metadata_block,
    render_plan_body_block,
    replace_metadata_block_in_body,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import (
    BRANCH_NAME,
    CREATED_AT,
    CREATED_BY,
    CREATED_FROM_SESSION,
    CREATED_FROM_WORKFLOW_RUN_URL,
    LAST_DISPATCHED_AT,
    LAST_DISPATCHED_NODE_ID,
    LAST_DISPATCHED_RUN_ID,
    LAST_LEARN_AT,
    LAST_LEARN_SESSION,
    LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT,
    LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_EVENT,
    LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_SESSION,
    LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_USER,
    LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT,
    LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_RUN_ID,
    LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_SESSION_ID,
    LAST_SESSION_AT,
    LAST_SESSION_BRANCH,
    LAST_SESSION_ID,
    LAST_SESSION_SOURCE,
    LEARN_MATERIALS_BRANCH,
    LEARN_PLAN_ISSUE,
    LEARN_PLAN_PR,
    LEARN_RUN_ID,
    LEARN_STATUS,
    LEARNED_FROM_ISSUE,
    LIFECYCLE_STAGE,
    OBJECTIVE_ISSUE,
    PLAN_COMMENT_ID,
    SCHEMA_VERSION,
    SOURCE_REPO,
    WORKTREE_NAME,
    LearnStatusValue,
    PlanHeaderSchema,
    SessionSourceValue,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import MetadataBlock


def create_plan_header_block(
    *,
    created_at: str,
    created_by: str,
    worktree_name: str | None,
    branch_name: str | None,
    plan_comment_id: int | None,
    last_dispatched_run_id: str | None,
    last_dispatched_node_id: str | None,
    last_dispatched_at: str | None,
    last_local_impl_at: str | None,
    last_local_impl_event: str | None,
    last_local_impl_session: str | None,
    last_local_impl_user: str | None,
    last_remote_impl_at: str | None,
    last_remote_impl_run_id: str | None,
    last_remote_impl_session_id: str | None,
    source_repo: str | None,
    objective_issue: int | None,
    created_from_session: str | None,
    created_from_workflow_run_url: str | None,
    last_learn_session: str | None,
    last_learn_at: str | None,
    learn_status: LearnStatusValue | None,
    learn_plan_issue: int | None,
    learn_plan_pr: int | None,
    learned_from_issue: int | None,
    lifecycle_stage: str | None,
) -> MetadataBlock:
    """Create a plan-header metadata block with validation.

    Args:
        created_at: ISO 8601 timestamp of plan creation
        created_by: GitHub username of plan creator
        worktree_name: Optional worktree name (set when worktree is created)
        branch_name: Optional git branch name for this plan
        plan_comment_id: Optional GitHub comment ID containing plan content
        last_dispatched_run_id: Optional workflow run ID (set by workflow)
        last_dispatched_node_id: Optional GraphQL node ID (set by workflow, for batch queries)
        last_dispatched_at: Optional dispatch timestamp (set by workflow)
        last_local_impl_at: Optional local implementation timestamp (set by plan-implement)
        last_local_impl_event: Optional event type ("started" or "ended")
        last_local_impl_session: Optional Claude Code session ID
        last_local_impl_user: Optional user who ran implementation
        last_remote_impl_at: Optional remote implementation timestamp (set by GitHub Actions)
        last_remote_impl_run_id: Optional GitHub Actions run ID for remote implementation
        last_remote_impl_session_id: Optional Claude Code session ID for remote implementation
        source_repo: For cross-repo plans, the repo where implementation happens
        objective_issue: Optional parent objective issue number
        created_from_session: Optional session ID that created this plan
        created_from_workflow_run_url: Optional workflow run URL that created this plan
        last_learn_session: Optional session ID that last invoked learn
        last_learn_at: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp of last learn invocation
        learn_status: Optional learning workflow status
        learn_plan_issue: Optional issue number of generated learn plan
        learn_plan_pr: Optional PR number that implemented the learn plan
        learned_from_issue: Optional parent plan issue number (for learn plans)
        lifecycle_stage: Optional lifecycle stage for the plan

    Returns:
        MetadataBlock with plan-header schema
    """
    schema = PlanHeaderSchema()

    data: dict[str, Any] = {
        SCHEMA_VERSION: "2",
        CREATED_AT: created_at,
        CREATED_BY: created_by,
        PLAN_COMMENT_ID: plan_comment_id,
        LAST_DISPATCHED_RUN_ID: last_dispatched_run_id,
        LAST_DISPATCHED_NODE_ID: last_dispatched_node_id,
        LAST_DISPATCHED_AT: last_dispatched_at,
        LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT: last_local_impl_at,
        LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_EVENT: last_local_impl_event,
        LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_SESSION: last_local_impl_session,
        LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_USER: last_local_impl_user,
        LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT: last_remote_impl_at,
        LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_RUN_ID: last_remote_impl_run_id,
        LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_SESSION_ID: last_remote_impl_session_id,
    }
    # Only include worktree_name if provided
    if worktree_name is not None:
        data[WORKTREE_NAME] = worktree_name

    # Only include branch_name if provided
    if branch_name is not None:
        data[BRANCH_NAME] = branch_name

    # Include source_repo for cross-repo plans
    if source_repo is not None:
        data[SOURCE_REPO] = source_repo

    # Include objective_issue if provided
    if objective_issue is not None:
        data[OBJECTIVE_ISSUE] = objective_issue

    # Include created_from_session if provided
    if created_from_session is not None:
        data[CREATED_FROM_SESSION] = created_from_session

    # Include created_from_workflow_run_url if provided
    if created_from_workflow_run_url is not None:
        data[CREATED_FROM_WORKFLOW_RUN_URL] = created_from_workflow_run_url

    # Include last_learn_session if provided
    if last_learn_session is not None:
        data[LAST_LEARN_SESSION] = last_learn_session

    # Include last_learn_at if provided
    if last_learn_at is not None:
        data[LAST_LEARN_AT] = last_learn_at

    # Include learn_status if provided
    if learn_status is not None:
        data[LEARN_STATUS] = learn_status

    # Include learn_plan_issue if provided
    if learn_plan_issue is not None:
        data[LEARN_PLAN_ISSUE] = learn_plan_issue

    # Include learn_plan_pr if provided
    if learn_plan_pr is not None:
        data[LEARN_PLAN_PR] = learn_plan_pr

    # Include learned_from_issue if provided
    if learned_from_issue is not None:
        data[LEARNED_FROM_ISSUE] = learned_from_issue

    # Include lifecycle_stage if provided
    if lifecycle_stage is not None:
        data[LIFECYCLE_STAGE] = lifecycle_stage

    return create_metadata_block(
        key=schema.get_key(),
        data=data,
        schema=schema,
    )


def format_plan_header_body(
    *,
    created_at: str,
    created_by: str,
    worktree_name: str | None,
    branch_name: str | None,
    plan_comment_id: int | None,
    last_dispatched_run_id: str | None,
    last_dispatched_node_id: str | None,
    last_dispatched_at: str | None,
    last_local_impl_at: str | None,
    last_local_impl_event: str | None,
    last_local_impl_session: str | None,
    last_local_impl_user: str | None,
    last_remote_impl_at: str | None,
    last_remote_impl_run_id: str | None,
    last_remote_impl_session_id: str | None,
    source_repo: str | None,
    objective_issue: int | None,
    created_from_session: str | None,
    created_from_workflow_run_url: str | None,
    last_learn_session: str | None,
    last_learn_at: str | None,
    learn_status: LearnStatusValue | None,
    learn_plan_issue: int | None,
    learn_plan_pr: int | None,
    learned_from_issue: int | None,
    lifecycle_stage: str | None,
) -> str:
    """Format issue body with only metadata (schema version 2).

    Creates an issue body containing just the plan-header metadata block.
    This is designed for fast querying - plan content goes in the first comment.

    Args:
        created_at: ISO 8601 timestamp of plan creation
        created_by: GitHub username of plan creator
        worktree_name: Optional worktree name (set when worktree is created)
        branch_name: Optional git branch name for this plan
        plan_comment_id: Optional GitHub comment ID containing plan content
        last_dispatched_run_id: Optional workflow run ID
        last_dispatched_node_id: Optional GraphQL node ID (for batch queries)
        last_dispatched_at: Optional dispatch timestamp
        last_local_impl_at: Optional local implementation timestamp
        last_local_impl_event: Optional event type ("started" or "ended")
        last_local_impl_session: Optional Claude Code session ID
        last_local_impl_user: Optional user who ran implementation
        last_remote_impl_at: Optional remote implementation timestamp
        last_remote_impl_run_id: Optional GitHub Actions run ID for remote implementation
        last_remote_impl_session_id: Optional Claude Code session ID for remote implementation
        source_repo: For cross-repo plans, the repo where implementation happens
        objective_issue: Optional parent objective issue number
        created_from_session: Optional session ID that created this plan
        created_from_workflow_run_url: Optional workflow run URL that created this plan
        last_learn_session: Optional session ID that last invoked learn
        last_learn_at: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp of last learn invocation
        learn_status: Optional learning workflow status
        learn_plan_issue: Optional issue number of generated learn plan
        learn_plan_pr: Optional PR number that implemented the learn plan
        learned_from_issue: Optional parent plan issue number (for learn plans)
        lifecycle_stage: Optional lifecycle stage for the plan

    Returns:
        Issue body string with metadata block only
    """
    block = create_plan_header_block(
        created_at=created_at,
        created_by=created_by,
        worktree_name=worktree_name,
        branch_name=branch_name,
        plan_comment_id=plan_comment_id,
        last_dispatched_run_id=last_dispatched_run_id,
        last_dispatched_node_id=last_dispatched_node_id,
        last_dispatched_at=last_dispatched_at,
        last_local_impl_at=last_local_impl_at,
        last_local_impl_event=last_local_impl_event,
        last_local_impl_session=last_local_impl_session,
        last_local_impl_user=last_local_impl_user,
        last_remote_impl_at=last_remote_impl_at,
        last_remote_impl_run_id=last_remote_impl_run_id,
        last_remote_impl_session_id=last_remote_impl_session_id,
        source_repo=source_repo,
        objective_issue=objective_issue,
        created_from_session=created_from_session,
        created_from_workflow_run_url=created_from_workflow_run_url,
        last_learn_session=last_learn_session,
        last_learn_at=last_learn_at,
        learn_status=learn_status,
        learn_plan_issue=learn_plan_issue,
        learn_plan_pr=learn_plan_pr,
        learned_from_issue=learned_from_issue,
        lifecycle_stage=lifecycle_stage,
    )

    return render_metadata_block(block)


def format_plan_content_comment(plan_content: str) -> str:
    """Format plan content for the first comment (schema version 2).

    Wraps plan content in collapsible metadata block for GitHub display.

    Args:
        plan_content: The full plan markdown content

    Returns:
        Comment body with plan wrapped in collapsible metadata block
    """
    block = create_plan_body_block(plan_content.strip())
    return render_plan_body_block(block)


def extract_plan_from_comment(comment_body: str) -> str | None:
    """Extract plan content from a comment with plan-body metadata block.

    Extracts from both:
    - New format: <!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-body --> with <details>
    - Old format: <!-- erk:plan-content --> (backward compatibility)

    Args:
        comment_body: Comment body potentially containing plan content

    Returns:
        Extracted plan content, or None if markers not found
    """
    # Import here to avoid circular dependency
    from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import extract_raw_metadata_blocks

    # Try new format first (plan-body metadata block)
    raw_blocks = extract_raw_metadata_blocks(comment_body)
    for block in raw_blocks:
        if block.key == "plan-body":
            # Extract content from <details> structure
            # The plan-body block uses <strong> tags in summary (not <code>)
            # Accept both <details> and <details open>
            pattern = r"<details(?:\s+open)?>\s*<summary>.*?</summary>\s*(.*?)\s*</details>"
            match = re.search(pattern, block.body, re.DOTALL)
            if match:
                return match.group(1).strip()

    # Fall back to old format (backward compatibility)
    pattern = r"<!-- erk:plan-content -->\s*(.*?)\s*<!-- /erk:plan-content -->"
    match = re.search(pattern, comment_body, re.DOTALL)

    if match is None:
        return None

    return match.group(1).strip()


def update_plan_header_dispatch(
    issue_body: str,
    run_id: str,
    node_id: str,
    dispatched_at: str,
) -> str:
    """Update dispatch fields in plan-header metadata block.

    Uses Python YAML parsing for robustness (not regex).
    This function reads the existing plan-header block, updates the
    dispatch fields, and re-renders the entire body.

    Args:
        issue_body: Current issue body containing plan-header block
        run_id: Workflow run ID to set
        node_id: GraphQL node ID to set (for batch queries)
        dispatched_at: ISO 8601 timestamp of dispatch

    Returns:
        Updated issue body with new dispatch fields

    Raises:
        ValueError: If plan-header block not found or invalid
    """
    # Extract existing plan-header block
    block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, "plan-header")
    if block is None:
        raise ValueError("plan-header block not found in issue body")

    # Update dispatch fields
    updated_data = dict(block.data)
    updated_data[LAST_DISPATCHED_RUN_ID] = run_id
    updated_data[LAST_DISPATCHED_NODE_ID] = node_id
    updated_data[LAST_DISPATCHED_AT] = dispatched_at

    # Validate updated data
    schema = PlanHeaderSchema()
    schema.validate(updated_data)

    # Create new block and render
    new_block = MetadataBlock(key="plan-header", data=updated_data)
    new_block_content = render_metadata_block(new_block)

    # Replace block in full body
    return replace_metadata_block_in_body(issue_body, "plan-header", new_block_content)


def extract_plan_header_dispatch_info(
    issue_body: str,
) -> tuple[str | None, str | None, str | None]:
    """Extract dispatch info from plan-header block.

    Args:
        issue_body: Issue body containing plan-header block

    Returns:
        Tuple of (last_dispatched_run_id, last_dispatched_node_id, last_dispatched_at)
        All are None if block not found or fields not present
    """
    block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, "plan-header")
    if block is None:
        return (None, None, None)

    run_id = block.data.get(LAST_DISPATCHED_RUN_ID)
    node_id = block.data.get(LAST_DISPATCHED_NODE_ID)
    dispatched_at = block.data.get(LAST_DISPATCHED_AT)

    return (run_id, node_id, dispatched_at)


def extract_plan_header_worktree_name(issue_body: str) -> str | None:
    """Extract worktree_name from plan-header block.

    Args:
        issue_body: Issue body containing plan-header block

    Returns:
        worktree_name if found, None if block is missing or field is unset
    """
    block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, "plan-header")
    if block is None:
        return None

    return block.data.get(WORKTREE_NAME)


def extract_plan_header_branch_name(issue_body: str) -> str | None:
    """Extract branch_name from plan-header block.

    Args:
        issue_body: Issue body containing plan-header block

    Returns:
        branch_name if found, None if block is missing or field is unset
    """
    block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, "plan-header")
    if block is None:
        return None

    return block.data.get(BRANCH_NAME)


def extract_plan_header_comment_id(issue_body: str) -> int | None:
    """Extract plan_comment_id from plan-header block.

    Args:
        issue_body: Issue body containing plan-header block

    Returns:
        plan_comment_id if found, None if block is missing or field is unset
    """
    block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, "plan-header")
    if block is None:
        return None

    return block.data.get(PLAN_COMMENT_ID)


def update_plan_header_comment_id(
    issue_body: str,
    comment_id: int,
) -> str:
    """Update plan_comment_id field in plan-header metadata block.

    Uses Python YAML parsing for robustness (not regex).
    This function reads the existing plan-header block, updates the
    plan_comment_id field, and re-renders the entire body.

    Args:
        issue_body: Current issue body containing plan-header block
        comment_id: GitHub comment ID containing the plan content

    Returns:
        Updated issue body with new plan_comment_id field

    Raises:
        ValueError: If plan-header block not found or invalid
    """
    # Extract existing plan-header block
    block = find_metadata_block(issue_body, "plan-header")
    if block is None:
        raise ValueError("plan-header block not found in issue body")

    # Update plan_comment_id field
    updated_data = dict(block.data)
    updated_data[PLAN_COMMENT_ID] = comment_id

    # Validate updated data
    schema = PlanHeaderSchema()
    schema.validate(updated_data)

    # Create new block and render
    new_block = MetadataBlock(key="plan-header", data=updated_data)
    new_block_content = render_metadata_block(new_block)

    # Replace block in full body
    return replace_metadata_block_in_body(issue_body, "plan-header", new_block_content)


def update_plan_header_local_impl(
    issue_body: str,
    local_impl_at: str,
) -> str:
    """Update last_local_impl_at field in plan-header metadata block.

    Uses Python YAML parsing for robustness (not regex).
    This function reads the existing plan-header block, updates the
    local_impl_at field, and re-renders the entire body.

"""Unit tests for plan_content module."""

from pathlib import Path

from erk_shared.gateway.claude_installation.fake import FakeClaudeInstallation
from erk_shared.plan_store.plan_content import extract_title_from_plan, resolve_plan_content

# extract_title_from_plan tests


def test_extract_title_from_plan_returns_first_heading() -> None:
    """Verify first markdown heading is extracted as title."""
    content = "# My Feature Plan\n\nSome description"

    result = extract_title_from_plan(content)

    assert result == "My Feature Plan"


def test_extract_title_from_plan_strips_whitespace() -> None:
    """Verify whitespace around title is stripped."""
    content = "#   Spaced Title   \n\nContent"

    result = extract_title_from_plan(content)

    assert result == "Spaced Title"


def test_extract_title_from_plan_returns_untitled_when_no_heading() -> None:
    """Verify fallback to 'Untitled Plan' when no heading found."""
    content = "Some text without any headings\n\nMore text"

    result = extract_title_from_plan(content)

    assert result == "Untitled Plan"


def test_extract_title_from_plan_ignores_h2_headings() -> None:
    """Verify only h1 headings are considered."""
    content = "## H2 Heading\n\n### H3 Heading"

    result = extract_title_from_plan(content)

    assert result == "Untitled Plan"


def test_extract_title_from_plan_skips_leading_blank_lines() -> None:
    """Verify heading is found even after leading blank lines."""
    content = "\n\n\n# Heading After Blanks\n\nContent"

    result = extract_title_from_plan(content)

    assert result == "Heading After Blanks"


def test_extract_title_from_plan_empty_string() -> None:
    """Verify empty string returns fallback."""
    result = extract_title_from_plan("")

    assert result == "Untitled Plan"


# resolve_plan_content tests


def test_resolve_plan_content_plan_file_takes_priority(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    """Verify plan_file parameter has highest priority."""
    plan_file = tmp_path / "explicit-plan.md"
    plan_file.write_text("# Explicit Plan", encoding="utf-8")

    fake_claude = FakeClaudeInstallation.for_test(
        plans={"other": "# Other Plan"},
    )

    result = resolve_plan_content(
        plan_file=plan_file,
        session_id="test-session",
        repo_root=tmp_path,
        claude_installation=fake_claude,
        cwd=tmp_path,
    )

    assert result == "# Explicit Plan"


def test_resolve_plan_content_scratch_dir_over_claude_plans(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    """Verify scratch directory plan takes priority over claude_installation."""
    session_id = "test-session"
    scratch_dir = tmp_path / ".erk" / "scratch" / "sessions" / session_id
    scratch_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
    (scratch_dir / "plan.md").write_text("# Scratch Plan", encoding="utf-8")

    fake_claude = FakeClaudeInstallation.for_test(
        plans={"other": "# Claude Plan"},
    )

    result = resolve_plan_content(
        plan_file=None,
        session_id=session_id,
        repo_root=tmp_path,
        claude_installation=fake_claude,
        cwd=tmp_path,
    )

    assert result == "# Scratch Plan"


def test_resolve_plan_content_falls_back_to_claude_installation(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    """Verify fallback to claude_installation when no scratch plan exists."""
    fake_claude = FakeClaudeInstallation.for_test(
        plans={"fallback": "# Claude Plan"},
    )

    result = resolve_plan_content(
        plan_file=None,
        session_id="no-scratch-session",
        repo_root=tmp_path,
        claude_installation=fake_claude,
        cwd=tmp_path,
    )

    assert result == "# Claude Plan"


def test_resolve_plan_content_returns_none_when_no_plan(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    """Verify None is returned when no plan found anywhere."""
    fake_claude = FakeClaudeInstallation.for_test()

    result = resolve_plan_content(
        plan_file=None,
        session_id="empty-session",
        repo_root=tmp_path,
        claude_installation=fake_claude,
        cwd=tmp_path,
    )

    assert result is None


def test_resolve_plan_content_no_session_id_uses_claude_installation(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    """Verify scratch is skipped when session_id is None."""
    fake_claude = FakeClaudeInstallation.for_test(
        plans={"plan": "# No Session Plan"},
    )

    result = resolve_plan_content(
        plan_file=None,
        session_id=None,
        repo_root=tmp_path,
        claude_installation=fake_claude,
        cwd=tmp_path,
    )

    assert result == "# No Session Plan"

1→"""Tests for one-shot dispatch shared logic."""
     2→
     3→from click.testing import CliRunner
     4→
     5→from erk.cli.cli import cli
     6→from erk.cli.commands.one_shot_dispatch import (
     7→    OneShotDispatchParams,
     8→    dispatch_one_shot,
     9→)
    10→from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit
    11→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError
    12→from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub
    13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
    14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound
    15→from erk_shared.gateway.time.fake import FakeTime
    16→from erk_shared.plan_store.draft_pr import DraftPRPlanBackend
    17→from tests.test_utils.context_builders import build_workspace_test_context
    18→from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_isolated_fs_env
    19→
    20→
    21→def test_dispatch_happy_path() -> None:
    22→    """Test dispatch creates skeleton issue, branch with P<N>- prefix, PR, and triggers workflow."""
    23→    runner = CliRunner()
    24→    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
    25→        env.setup_repo_structure()
    26→
    27→        git = FakeGit(
    28→            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
    29→            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
    30→            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
    31→            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
    32→        )
    33→        issues = FakeGitHubIssues()
    34→        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True, issues_gateway=issues)
    35→
    36→        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github, issues=issues)
    37→
    38→        params = OneShotDispatchParams(
    39→            prompt="fix the import in config.py",
    40→            model=None,
    41→            extra_workflow_inputs={},
    42→        )
    43→
    44→        result = dispatch_one_shot(ctx, params=params, dry_run=False)
    45→
    46→        assert result is not None
    47→        # Branch should have P<N>- prefix (skeleton issue created first)
    48→        assert result.branch_name.startswith("P1-")
    49→
    50→        # Verify skeleton plan issue was created
    51→        assert len(issues.created_issues) == 1
    52→        title, _body, labels = issues.created_issues[0]
    53→        assert "[erk-plan]" in title
    54→        assert "erk-plan" in labels
    55→
    56→        # Verify branch was created
    57→        assert len(git.created_branches) == 1
    58→        assert git.created_branches[0][2] == "main"  # start_point is trunk
    59→
    60→        # Verify .erk/impl-context/prompt.md was committed directly to branch (no checkout)
    61→        assert len(git.branch_commits) == 1
    62→        assert git.branch_commits[0].files == {
    63→            ".erk/impl-context/prompt.md": "fix the import in config.py\n",
    64→        }
    65→        assert git.branch_commits[0].branch.startswith("P1-")
    66→
    67→        # Verify push to remote
    68→        assert len(git.pushed_branches) == 1
    69→
    70→        # Verify PR was created with closing reference
    71→        assert len(github.created_prs) == 1
    72→        _branch, _title, pr_body, base, draft = github.created_prs[0]
    73→        assert draft is True
    74→        assert base == "main"
    75→        assert "Closes #1" in pr_body
    76→
    77→        # Verify workflow was triggered with plan_issue_number and plan_backend
    78→        assert len(github.triggered_workflows) == 1
    79→        workflow, inputs = github.triggered_workflows[0]
    80→        assert workflow == "one-shot.yml"
    81→        assert inputs["prompt"] == "fix the import in config.py"
    82→        assert inputs["plan_issue_number"] == "1"
    83→        assert inputs["plan_backend"] == "github"
    84→
    85→        # Verify PR body was updated with workflow run link and closing reference
    86→        assert len(github.updated_pr_bodies) == 1
    87→        _pr_num, updated_body = github.updated_pr_bodies[0]
    88→        assert "**Workflow run:**" in updated_body
    89→        assert "https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/" in updated_body
    90→        assert "Closes #1" in updated_body
    91→
    92→        # Verify dispatch metadata was written to plan issue
    93→        issue_info = issues.get_issue(env.cwd, 1)
    94→        assert not isinstance(issue_info, IssueNotFound)
    95→        assert "last_dispatched_run_id" in issue_info.body
    96→        assert "last_dispatched_node_id" in issue_info.body
    97→        assert "last_dispatched_at" in issue_info.body
    98→
    99→        # Verify we're back on original branch
   100→        assert git.branch.get_current_branch(env.cwd) == "main"
   101→
   102→
   103→def test_dispatch_with_extra_inputs() -> None:
   104→    """Test extra_workflow_inputs in workflow trigger and skeleton links to objective."""
   105→    runner = CliRunner()
   106→    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
   107→        env.setup_repo_structure()
   108→
   109→        git = FakeGit(
   110→            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
   111→            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
   112→            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
   113→            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
   114→        )
   115→        issues = FakeGitHubIssues()
   116→        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True, issues_gateway=issues)
   117→
   118→        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github, issues=issues)
   119→
   120→        params = OneShotDispatchParams(
   121→            prompt="implement step 1.1",
   122→            model=None,
   123→            extra_workflow_inputs={
   124→                "objective_issue": "42",
   125→                "node_id": "1.1",
   126→            },
   127→        )
   128→
   129→        result = dispatch_one_shot(ctx, params=params, dry_run=False)
   130→
   131→        assert result is not None
   132→        # Branch should have P<N>- prefix
   133→        assert result.branch_name.startswith("P1-")
   134→
   135→        # Verify extra inputs are in workflow trigger
   136→        _workflow, inputs = github.triggered_workflows[0]
   137→        assert inputs["objective_issue"] == "42"
   138→        assert inputs["node_id"] == "1.1"
   139→        assert inputs["prompt"] == "implement step 1.1"
   140→        assert inputs["plan_issue_number"] == "1"
   141→
   142→
   143→def test_dispatch_dry_run() -> None:
   144→    """Test dispatch_one_shot dry_run outputs info without mutations."""
   145→    runner = CliRunner()
   146→    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
   147→        env.setup_repo_structure()
   148→
   149→        git = FakeGit(
   150→            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
   151→            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
   152→            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
   153→            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
   154→        )
   155→        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True)
   156→
   157→        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github)
   158→
   159→        # Invoke via CLI to capture user_output via CliRunner
   160→        result = runner.invoke(
   161→            cli,
   162→            ["one-shot", "add type hints", "--dry-run", "-m", "opus"],
   163→            obj=ctx,
   164→            catch_exceptions=False,
   165→        )
   166→
   167→        assert result.exit_code == 0
   168→        assert "Dry-run mode:" in result.output
   169→        assert "add type hints" in result.output
   170→
   171→        # Verify no mutations occurred (skeleton issue NOT created in dry-run)
   172→        assert len(git.created_branches) == 0
   173→        assert len(git.pushed_branches) == 0
   174→        assert len(github.created_prs) == 0
   175→        assert len(github.triggered_workflows) == 0
   176→
   177→
   178→def test_dispatch_stays_on_current_branch_on_error() -> None:
   179→    """Test that we stay on current branch when push fails (no checkout = nothing to restore)."""
   180→    runner = CliRunner()
   181→    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
   182→        env.setup_repo_structure()
   183→
   184→        git = FakeGit(
   185→            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
   186→            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
   187→            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
   188→            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
   189→            push_to_remote_error=PushError(message="network error"),
   190→        )
   191→        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True)
   192→
   193→        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github)
   194→
   195→        result = runner.invoke(

   206→
   207→
   208→def test_dispatch_creates_skeleton_plan_issue() -> None:
   209→    """Test that skeleton plan issue is created with correct content and metadata."""
   210→    runner = CliRunner()
   211→    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
   212→        env.setup_repo_structure()
   213→
   214→        git = FakeGit(

"""Tests for one-shot dispatch shared logic."""

from click.testing import CliRunner

from erk.cli.cli import cli
from erk.cli.commands.one_shot_dispatch import (
    OneShotDispatchParams,
    dispatch_one_shot,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit
from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError
from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub
from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound
from erk_shared.gateway.time.fake import FakeTime
from erk_shared.plan_store.draft_pr import DraftPRPlanBackend
from tests.test_utils.context_builders import build_workspace_test_context
from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_isolated_fs_env


def test_dispatch_happy_path() -> None:
    """Test dispatch creates skeleton issue, branch with P<N>- prefix, PR, and triggers workflow."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        env.setup_repo_structure()

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        )
        issues = FakeGitHubIssues()
        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True, issues_gateway=issues)

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github, issues=issues)

        params = OneShotDispatchParams(
            prompt="fix the import in config.py",
            model=None,
            extra_workflow_inputs={},
        )

        result = dispatch_one_shot(ctx, params=params, dry_run=False)

        assert result is not None
        # Branch should have P<N>- prefix (skeleton issue created first)
        assert result.branch_name.startswith("P1-")

        # Verify skeleton plan issue was created
        assert len(issues.created_issues) == 1
        title, _body, labels = issues.created_issues[0]
        assert "[erk-plan]" in title
        assert "erk-plan" in labels

        # Verify branch was created
        assert len(git.created_branches) == 1
        assert git.created_branches[0][2] == "main"  # start_point is trunk

        # Verify .erk/impl-context/prompt.md was committed directly to branch (no checkout)
        assert len(git.branch_commits) == 1
        assert git.branch_commits[0].files == {
            ".erk/impl-context/prompt.md": "fix the import in config.py\n",
        }
        assert git.branch_commits[0].branch.startswith("P1-")

        # Verify push to remote
        assert len(git.pushed_branches) == 1

        # Verify PR was created with closing reference
        assert len(github.created_prs) == 1
        _branch, _title, pr_body, base, draft = github.created_prs[0]
        assert draft is True
        assert base == "main"
        assert "Closes #1" in pr_body

        # Verify workflow was triggered with plan_issue_number and plan_backend
        assert len(github.triggered_workflows) == 1
        workflow, inputs = github.triggered_workflows[0]
        assert workflow == "one-shot.yml"
        assert inputs["prompt"] == "fix the import in config.py"
        assert inputs["plan_issue_number"] == "1"
        assert inputs["plan_backend"] == "github"

        # Verify PR body was updated with workflow run link and closing reference
        assert len(github.updated_pr_bodies) == 1
        _pr_num, updated_body = github.updated_pr_bodies[0]
        assert "**Workflow run:**" in updated_body
        assert "https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/" in updated_body
        assert "Closes #1" in updated_body

        # Verify dispatch metadata was written to plan issue
        issue_info = issues.get_issue(env.cwd, 1)
        assert not isinstance(issue_info, IssueNotFound)
        assert "last_dispatched_run_id" in issue_info.body
        assert "last_dispatched_node_id" in issue_info.body
        assert "last_dispatched_at" in issue_info.body

        # Verify we're back on original branch
        assert git.branch.get_current_branch(env.cwd) == "main"


def test_dispatch_with_extra_inputs() -> None:
    """Test extra_workflow_inputs in workflow trigger and skeleton links to objective."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        env.setup_repo_structure()

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        )
        issues = FakeGitHubIssues()
        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True, issues_gateway=issues)

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github, issues=issues)

        params = OneShotDispatchParams(
            prompt="implement step 1.1",
            model=None,
            extra_workflow_inputs={
                "objective_issue": "42",
                "node_id": "1.1",
            },
        )

        result = dispatch_one_shot(ctx, params=params, dry_run=False)

        assert result is not None
        # Branch should have P<N>- prefix
        assert result.branch_name.startswith("P1-")

        # Verify extra inputs are in workflow trigger
        _workflow, inputs = github.triggered_workflows[0]
        assert inputs["objective_issue"] == "42"
        assert inputs["node_id"] == "1.1"
        assert inputs["prompt"] == "implement step 1.1"
        assert inputs["plan_issue_number"] == "1"


def test_dispatch_dry_run() -> None:
    """Test dispatch_one_shot dry_run outputs info without mutations."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        env.setup_repo_structure()

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        )
        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True)

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github)

        # Invoke via CLI to capture user_output via CliRunner
        result = runner.invoke(
            cli,
            ["one-shot", "add type hints", "--dry-run", "-m", "opus"],
            obj=ctx,
            catch_exceptions=False,
        )

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        assert "Dry-run mode:" in result.output
        assert "add type hints" in result.output

        # Verify no mutations occurred (skeleton issue NOT created in dry-run)
        assert len(git.created_branches) == 0
        assert len(git.pushed_branches) == 0
        assert len(github.created_prs) == 0
        assert len(github.triggered_workflows) == 0


def test_dispatch_stays_on_current_branch_on_error() -> None:
    """Test that we stay on current branch when push fails (no checkout = nothing to restore)."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        env.setup_repo_structure()

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            push_to_remote_error=PushError(message="network error"),
        )
        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True)

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github)

        result = runner.invoke(
            cli,
            ["one-shot", "fix the import in config.py"],
            obj=ctx,
        )

        # Verify command failed
        assert result.exit_code != 0

        # Verify we're still on original branch (no checkout occurred)
        assert git.branch.get_current_branch(env.cwd) == "main"


def test_dispatch_creates_skeleton_plan_issue() -> None:
    """Test that skeleton plan issue is created with correct content and metadata."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        env.setup_repo_structure()

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        )
        issues = FakeGitHubIssues()
        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True, issues_gateway=issues)

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github, issues=issues)

        params = OneShotDispatchParams(
            prompt="add user authentication",
            model=None,
            extra_workflow_inputs={},
        )

        dispatch_one_shot(ctx, params=params, dry_run=False)

        # Verify skeleton issue was created with expected content
        assert len(issues.created_issues) == 1
        title, _body, labels = issues.created_issues[0]
        assert "[erk-plan]" in title
        assert "erk-plan" in labels

        # Verify plan comment was added (create_plan_issue adds plan as comment)
        # Plus queued event comment from dispatch
        assert len(issues.added_comments) == 2
        issue_number, comment_body, _comment_id = issues.added_comments[0]
        assert issue_number == 1
        assert "One-shot" in comment_body
        assert "add user authentication" in comment_body

        # Verify queued event comment
        issue_number, queued_body, _comment_id = issues.added_comments[1]
        assert issue_number == 1
        assert "One-Shot Dispatched" in queued_body
        assert "add user authentication" in queued_body


def test_dispatch_posts_queued_event_comment() -> None:
    """Test dispatch posts a queued event comment with workflow run URL and prompt."""

def test_dispatch_draft_pr_lifecycle() -> None:
    """Test full draft_pr dispatch: no skeleton issue, plnd/ branch, PR with metadata block.

    In draft_pr mode, the draft PR IS the plan entity. The dispatch should:
    - NOT create a skeleton issue
    - Use plnd/ branch naming
    - Create a draft PR with plan-header metadata block
    - Set plan_issue_number = pr_number for downstream code
    - Write dispatch metadata to the PR (the plan entity)
    - Post queued event comment to the PR
    """
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner) as env:
        env.setup_repo_structure()

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        )
        issues = FakeGitHubIssues()
        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True, issues_gateway=issues)

        # Explicitly use DraftPRPlanBackend so ctx.plan_backend.get_provider_name()
        # returns "github-draft-pr" and dispatch takes the draft_pr path.
        plan_store = DraftPRPlanBackend(github, issues, time=FakeTime())

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env, git=git, github=github, issues=issues, plan_store=plan_store
        )

        params = OneShotDispatchParams(
            prompt="fix the import in config.py",
            model=None,
            extra_workflow_inputs={},
        )

        result = dispatch_one_shot(ctx, params=params, dry_run=False)

        assert result is not None

        # No skeleton issue created — draft_pr skips issue creation
        assert len(issues.created_issues) == 0

        # Branch uses plnd/ prefix (not P<N>-)
        assert result.branch_name.startswith("plnd/")
        assert not result.branch_name.startswith("P")

        # PR created with plan-header metadata block
        assert len(github.created_prs) == 1
        _branch, _title, pr_body, base, draft = github.created_prs[0]
        assert draft is True
        assert base == "main"
        assert "plan-header" in pr_body
        assert "lifecycle_stage: prompted" in pr_body
        assert "fix the import in config.py" in pr_body

        # No Closes #N in PR body (self-referential for draft_pr)
        assert "Closes #" not in pr_body

        # erk-plan label added to PR
        pr_number = result.pr_number
        assert (pr_number, "erk-plan") in github.added_labels

        # Workflow inputs include plan_issue_number = pr_number and plan_backend
        assert len(github.triggered_workflows) == 1
        _workflow, inputs = github.triggered_workflows[0]
        assert inputs["plan_issue_number"] == str(pr_number)
        assert inputs["plan_backend"] == "draft_pr"

        # Dispatch metadata written to the PR (the plan entity).
        # The PR body is updated with footer, then dispatch metadata written
        # via plan_backend.update_metadata targeting the PR number.
        # updated_pr_bodies includes: (1) footer update, (2) dispatch metadata update
        assert len(github.updated_pr_bodies) >= 2
        # The dispatch metadata update should contain last_dispatched_run_id
        last_body = github.updated_pr_bodies[-1][1]
        assert "last_dispatched_run_id" in last_body

        # Queued event comment is best-effort. In real GitHub, PRs are issues
        # and add_comment works on PR numbers. In the fake, PRs and issues are
        # separate, so the comment may fail. This is acceptable — dispatch
        # metadata is the critical operation.


def test_dispatch_long_prompt_truncates_workflow_input() -> None:
    """Test that long prompts are truncated in workflow input but committed in full."""
    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner) as env:
        env.setup_repo_structure()

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
        )
        issues = FakeGitHubIssues()
        github = FakeGitHub(authenticated=True, issues_gateway=issues)

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, git=git, github=github, issues=issues)

        long_prompt = "x" * 1000

        params = OneShotDispatchParams(
            prompt=long_prompt,
            model=None,
            extra_workflow_inputs={},
        )

        result = dispatch_one_shot(ctx, params=params, dry_run=False)

        assert result is not None

        # Verify workflow input was truncated
        _workflow, inputs = github.triggered_workflows[0]
        assert len(inputs["prompt"]) < len(long_prompt)
        assert inputs["prompt"].endswith(
            "... (full prompt committed to .erk/impl-context/prompt.md)"
        )

        # Verify full prompt was committed directly to branch via branch_commits
        assert len(git.branch_commits) == 1
        assert git.branch_commits[0].files == {".erk/impl-context/prompt.md": long_prompt + "\n"}

   159→        issue_number = self._next_issue_number

   163→        url = f"https://github.com/test-owner/test-repo/issues/{issue_number}"

   165→        now = datetime.now(UTC)

   213→        """Update issue body in fake storage and track mutation.

   251→        """Update issue title in fake storage and track mutation.
   252→

   272→    def list_issues(
   273→        self,
   274→        *,

   279→    ) -> list[IssueInfo]:
   280→        """Query issues from fake storage.
   281→
   282→        Filters issues by labels (AND logic) and state.
   283→        """
   284→        issues = list(self._issues.values())
   285→
   286→        # Filter by labels (AND logic - issue must have ALL specified labels)
   287→        if labels:
   288→            label_set = set(labels)
   289→            issues = [issue for issue in issues if label_set.issubset(set(issue.labels))]
   290→
   291→        if state and state != "all":
   292→            state_upper = state.upper()
   293→            issues = [issue for issue in issues if issue.state == state_upper]

   295→        if limit is not None:

   336→            or empty list if no comments exist
   337→

   340→        """
   341→        if self._get_comments_error is not None:
   342→            raise RuntimeError(self._get_comments_error)
   343→        return self._comments_with_urls.get(number, [])

   345→    def ensure_label_exists(

   358→        """Ensure label is present on issue in fake storage (idempotent).
   359→
   360→        Raises:
   361→            RuntimeError: If issue number not found (simulates gh CLI error)
   362→        """
   363→        if issue_number not in self._issues:
   364→            msg = f"Issue #{issue_number} not found"
   365→            raise RuntimeError(msg)
   366→
   367→        # Get current issue and create updated version with new label (if not present)
   368→        current_issue = self._issues[issue_number]
   369→        if label not in current_issue.labels:
   370→            updated_labels = current_issue.labels + [label]
   371→            self._issues[issue_number] = IssueInfo(
   372→                number=current_issue.number,
   373→                title=current_issue.title,
   374→                body=current_issue.body,
   375→                state=current_issue.state,
   376→                url=current_issue.url,
   377→                labels=updated_labels,
   378→                assignees=current_issue.assignees,
   379→                created_at=current_issue.created_at,
   380→                updated_at=current_issue.updated_at,
   381→                author=current_issue.author,
   382→            )
   383→
   384→    def remove_label_from_issue(self, repo_root: Path, issue_number: int, label: str) -> None:
   385→        """Remove label from issue in fake storage.
   386→
   387→        Raises:
   388→            RuntimeError: If issue number not found (simulates gh CLI error)
   389→        """
   390→        if issue_number not in self._issues:
   391→            msg = f"Issue #{issue_number} not found"
   392→            raise RuntimeError(msg)
   393→
   394→        # Get current issue and create updated version without the label
   395→        current_issue = self._issues[issue_number]
   396→        if label in current_issue.labels:
   397→            updated_labels = [lbl for lbl in current_issue.labels if lbl != label]
   398→            self._issues[issue_number] = IssueInfo(
   399→                number=current_issue.number,
   400→                title=current_issue.title,
   401→                body=current_issue.body,
   402→                state=current_issue.state,
   403→                url=current_issue.url,
   404→                labels=updated_labels,
   405→                assignees=current_issue.assignees,
   406→                created_at=current_issue.created_at,
   407→                updated_at=current_issue.updated_at,
   408→                author=current_issue.author,
   409→            )
   410→
   411→    def close_issue(self, repo_root: Path, number: int) -> None:
   412→        """Close issue in fake storage.
   413→
   414→        Raises:
   415→            RuntimeError: If issue number not found (simulates gh CLI error)
   416→        """
   417→        if number not in self._issues:
   418→            msg = f"Issue #{number} not found"
   419→            raise RuntimeError(msg)
   420→
   421→        # Update issue state to closed
   422→        current_issue = self._issues[number]
   423→        self._issues[number] = IssueInfo(
   424→            number=current_issue.number,
   425→            title=current_issue.title,
   426→            body=current_issue.body,
   427→            state="closed",
   428→            url=current_issue.url,
   429→            labels=current_issue.labels,
   430→            assignees=current_issue.assignees,
   431→            created_at=current_issue.created_at,
   432→            updated_at=current_issue.updated_at,
   433→            author=current_issue.author,
   434→        )
   435→        self._closed_issues.append(number)
   436→
   437→    def get_current_username(self) -> str | None:
   438→        """Return configured username from constructor.
   439→
   440→        Returns:
   441→            Username configured in constructor (default: "testuser")
   442→        """
   443→        return self._username
   444→
   445→    def get_prs_referencing_issue(
   446→        self,
   447→        repo_root: Path,
   448→        issue_number: int,
   449→    ) -> list[PRReference]:
   450→        """Get PRs referencing issue from configured state.
   451→
   452→        Returns:
   453→            List of PRReference from pr_references constructor arg,
   454→            or empty list if no references configured for this issue.
   455→        """
   456→        return self._pr_references.get(issue_number, [])
   457→
   458→    def add_reaction_to_comment(
   459→        self,
   460→        repo_root: Path,
   461→        comment_id: int,
   462→        reaction: str,
   463→    ) -> None:
   464→        """Record reaction in mutation tracking.
   465→
   466→        Note: Does not validate comment_id exists. Real API is idempotent.
   467→
   468→        Raises:
   469→            RuntimeError: If add_reaction_error was set in constructor
   470→        """
   471→        if self._add_reaction_error is not None:
   472→            raise RuntimeError(self._add_reaction_error)
   473→        self._added_reactions.append((comment_id, reaction))
   474→
   475→    def update_comment(
   476→        self,
   477→        repo_root: Path,
   478→        comment_id: int,
   479→        body: str,
   480→    ) -> None:
   481→        """Record comment update in mutation tracking.
   482→
   483→        Note: Does not validate comment_id exists. Real API returns 404 for
   484→        non-existent comments.
   485→        """
   486→        self._updated_comments.append((comment_id, body))
   487→
